issues.title: Flutter TextField changes macOS NSColorPanel color.
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch the app
2. Change the color in the color panel to some color which is not black.
3. Click in the TextField.
4. Observe how the color of the color panel changes.
5. Change the color in the color panel again to something else.
6. Focus the TextField again.
7. Observe how the color can change to one of the previously set colors (repeat the last steps a few times to see this more clearly like in the video).

- Sometimes typing also seems to trigger the color change, but it is harder to reproduce.
- After defocusing the app and focusing it again, the behavior sometimes does not appear to happen again.

### Expected results

Interacting with the TextField does not impact the color panel.

### Actual results

The color panel is set to a different (often previous) color when focusing the TextField or typing in it.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

https://github.com/KevinBrendel/color_panel_textfield

</details>

</details>


### Logs

<details><summary>Logs</summary>

None

</details>

Thanks for the report @KevinBrendel 
I tried to verify this behavior but was unable to replicate it using the color panel. I tried by using Pages app on macOS which has color panel but it didn't work with Flutter app properly. Can you provide details on how are you using the color panel to use with Flutter macOS app ?

Thanks for your response @darshankawar.

The demo app is supposed to open the color panel on launch and then you can replicate the bug as described/shown in the video using the Color Panel created by the native Swift Code (see MainFlutterWindow.swift).

There might be a glitch where the app might not show the color panel on first launch and you need to launch it a second time for it to show up. I have pushed another commit that might make opening the color panel for the first time more reliable.

I was able to reproduce this locally on my machine; you're correct, launching a second time got it working (breaking?) for me. Taking a bit of a look.

Thanks for the repro!

Thanks for the update @KevinBrendel That helped me to replicate properly now.

issues.title: [iPad] Apple pencil input is not properly captured by the textfield toolbar
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Paste the sample code and run the app on your iPad.
2. Write `Hello Word` in TextField. (It doesn't matter whether you use a pencil or a keyboard)
3. Select `Hello World`. (It doesn’t matter whether you do it with a pencil or your fingers)
4. When you press the `Copy` button with your finger, copying works normally.
5. Pressing the `Copy` button with the **Apple Pencil** will paste, not copy.

### Expected results

It should be copied even if pressing the `Copy` button with the **Apple Pencil**.

### Actual results

I clicked copy, but it just pastes.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() async {
  runApp(
    const MaterialApp(
      home: HomePage(),
    ),
  );
}

class HomePage extends StatelessWidget {
  const HomePage({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const Scaffold(
      body: Center(
        child: Padding(
          padding: EdgeInsets.all(50),
          child: TextField(),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<summary>Video demonstration</summary>

[[Upload media here]](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/26322627/9c8c56c4-f0f5-4d03-8ea3-cc99f0748f52)

</details>

issues.title: iOS a11y text entry is incomplete
issues.body:
This is a follow-up for issue https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/12786.

As of https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/4575 we have partial support for a11y text entry on iOS.

What works:
 - When "tabbed" or "dragged" into
   - Text fields are announced as "Text field"
   - The hint "double tap to edit"
   - Content of text field is announced 
   - It is announced weather a text field is in edit mode
   - The insert mode (e.g. character mode) is not announced.
 - Entered characters are echoed back
 - After inserting space the last word is echoed back
 - Swiping up or down moves the courser
 - Selecting text with reverse-pinch

What remains to be done:
 - [ ] **No announcement** when a **currently edited** text field gains focus via the "tap" gesture
 - [ ] The position of the cursor is **not** announced 
 - [ ] Copy&paste does **not** work when rotor is in edit mode
 - [ ] TextFields for passwords (`secureTextEntry = YES`) are **not** announced as "secure text fields"
 - [ ] TextFields for passwords (`secureTextEntry = YES`) announce their content (e.g. "5 bullets") instead of the number of characters

/cc @goderbauer
comments: I just noticed that text fields for secure entry (e.g. Passwords) are also not announced correctly on iOS even though we set `secureTextEntry = YES` on the UITextInput. Added two points for this to the bullet list above.

>

Verified above features using latest stable and [code sample](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/14543#issuecomment-687107874) as provided earlier, below is the latest behavior of each:








> * **No announcement** when a **currently edited** text field gains focus via the "tap" gesture

This sometimes work and sometimes doesn't. When it works, it reads `textfield is editing, character mode, insertion point on end`. If we move the cursor to the start of the editing text, it reads `textfield is editing, character mode, insertion point at start`.

> * The position of the cursor is **not** announced

Currently it reads, `insertion point at start` or `insertion point at end` depending on the cursor position.



> * Copy&paste does **not** work when rotor is in edit mode

This still persists.



> * TextFields for passwords (`secureTextEntry = YES`) are **not** announced as "secure text fields"
> * [ ]  TextFields for passwords (`secureTextEntry = YES`) announce their content (e.g. "5 bullets") instead of the number of characters

These still persists as mentioned.

Based on the verification and indifferent behavior observed, I'll keep the issue open and update labels to reflect current and correct status of this issue.

issues.title: [Windows][Desktop]: textfield caret position is not consistent when traversing Chinese text
issues.body:
Below problems occurred on windows 10 desktop version.
- put a long Chinese article which consists of multiple paragraphs into a textfield, and try to move caret by using keyboard. And soon you will find some weird caret position problems. E.g. the caret positions will suddenly go to unexpected positions etc. This might be relevant to the next problem.

- If mouse is used, soemtimes you just cannot click into the blank line in between the paragraphs. Seems if the newline is pasted from outside or read from db then it cannnot be clicked into. If the newline is originally made in textfield, no problem.

- When you try to select a specific piece of text segment by mouse, the caret will sometimes quickly move over the selected segment leaving nothing selected.

- When I copied a article from a text editor (like emeditor) to textfield in flutter, all the characters become blocks. Then If I selected carefully from the very first character to the last and then paste into textfield, there will be no blocks at all. So I guess some special marks made all its trailing characters unrecognizable.

Tán Zhōngguó “yǔ” hé “wén” de wèntí, wǒ juéde zuìhǎo néng xiān liǎojiě yīxià zài Zhōngguó tōngyòng de yǔyán. Zhōngguó de zhǔyào yǔyán yǒu nǎxiē? Wèishénme wǒ shuō zhège, ér bù shuō nàge? Yīnwei huánjìng? Yīnwei bèi qiǎngpò? Yīnwei wǒ ài zhège yǔyán? Yīnwei yǒu bìyào? Yīnwei zhè ge yǔyán hěn zhòngyào? Yě xiǎngxiang shénme shì Zhōngguórén de gòngtóng yǔyán? Yòng yīge gòngtóng yǔyán yǒu bìyào ma? Weishenme? Biéde Hànyǔ de qùxiàng huì zěnmeyàng? Zhīdao zhège yǔyán de jīběn yīnjié (bù bàokuò shēngdiào) zhǐ yǒu 408 ge ma?

Tán wénhuà, tán chuántǒng, yě gāi xiǎngxiang zìjǐ dúle duōshao gǔdài hé xiàndài de Zhōngwén shū hé diǎnjí, duì Zhōngguó de lìshǐ, wénxué, zhéxué, sīxiǎng, měishù, yīxué, zōngjiào, pēngrèn, zhèngzhi, jīngjì, jiàoyu, mínsú, kējì, chuántǒng (bǐrú xiàojìng fùmǔ) děngděng liǎojiě duōshao. I took a quick look at this but haven't been able to figure out the root cause yet. When logging the gesture arena through `debugPrintGestureArenaDiagnostics = true` I see that when trying to drag the start handle, the gesture arena is registering two participants in the arena the start and end handles `PanGestureRecognizer`, and the end handle is winning by default.

When I explicitly passed `MaterialTextSelectionControls` to the `TextField`, dragging the start handle worked in the situation described, so this may be an issue in the implementation of `CupertinoTextSelectionControls`. When logging the gesture arena in this scenario the start handles `PanGestureRecognizer`  is participating in the gesture arena (in addition to the start handles `TapGestureRecognizer` which is expected).

issues.title: `TextField(textInputAction: TextInputAction.done)` in Android emulator does not accept Enter for `done`
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

When using `TextField(textInputAction: TextInputAction.done)`, if you run your app in the Android emulator, the Enter key on the on-screen keyboard will change into a checkmark. Clicking this does in fact cause the `TextField`'s `onSubmit` handler to be called. However, pressing Enter on the computer that the emulator is running on does not accept Enter as the `done` action.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

Since the Android virtual keyboard's Enter key is replaced with a checkmark, which calls `onSubmit`, I would expect the desktop computer's Enter key to do the same.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

As above.

Flutter version 3.7.5
Dart version 2.19.2
Android emulator on Linux
comments:
When checking this on the latest stable channel 3.7.7, I don't see the issue. It works as expected from my end. 

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/104349824/224649085-2f1e7738-d691-4d0d-972e-bc28a7f6598e.mov

Please retry on the latest stable/master channel. If the issue still reproduces, please provide a recorded video if possible. Also, which Android emulator are you using? (OS, model)

I retried on 3.7.7. I figured out what the issue is. This combination does not work:

```dart
                maxLines: 1,
                minLines: 1,
                keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
                textInputAction: TextInputAction.done,
```

`multiline` seems to suppress `done`, which I guess is understandable, except for the fact that only one line of text is allowed in this particular widget.

I didn't notice this was set, because the field does not accept newlines, given that `maxLines` is set to `1`.

I suggest updating the documentation for `TextInputAction.done` and other related constants to point out that the `Enter` key on a physical keyboard will not trigger the action in a `multiline` `TextField`.

I have been setting `keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline` on all text widgets, and relying on `maxLines` to determine if they are truly multiline or not. I see that this is not the correct approach under the current API. However, I suggest making the API more robust: I wonder if `keyboardType` should be interpreted as `text`, not `multiline`, if `maxLines` is set to 1. That would avoid this sort of confusion.

Thanks for the update. I can see the issue if using the above sample code. 

IMO, it seems to make little sense in practice (or is there a case where I don't know of yet) because when users want a TextField to enter multiple lines, they may not restrict the `maxLines` to 1. Instead, the case mentioned in issue #95985 (proposal) seems to be more reasonable. 

However, either using `TextInputType.multiline` with `maxLines: 1` or `maxLines: 5`, `onSubmitted` is also not invoked from the physical keyboard. If this is intentional, it should be updated on the documentation.

Labeling this issue for further insights from others. This issue is reproducible on the latest `stable` and `master` channels.

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

  runApp(const MyApp());
}

}
```

issues.title: Keyboard must be dismissed twice (Android / SwiftKey)
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample
2. Use [Microsoft Swiftkey](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.touchtype.swiftkey) as your keyboard
3. Focus the textarea, type and dismiss (fine)
4. Focus the textarea TWICE to activate the teardrop cursor*, the keyboard will need dismissing twice (bug)

_* I'm not sure of the correct term here_

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(
      title: 'Retrieve Text Input',
      home: MyCustomForm(),
    );
  }
}

class MyCustomForm extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyCustomForm({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  _MyCustomFormState createState() => _MyCustomFormState();
}

class _MyCustomFormState extends State<MyCustomForm> {
  final myController = TextEditingController();

  @override
  void dispose() {
    myController.dispose();
    super.dispose();
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: Center(
        child: TextField(
          controller: myController,
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

issues.title: [Web] When the input field is restricted to only allow numbers, switching to the Chinese input method results in the entered numbers being gradually deleted, and it also allows exceeding the maximum input limit.
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

flutter for web
1. Install Chinese input on a Windows computer.
2. Set the input to only support numbers and have a maximum length of 8 characters.
3. After completing the above steps, when entering a few numbers and switching to Chinese input mode, clicking in the input field causes the previously entered numbers to be gradually deleted, and it also allows exceeding the maximum input limit.

### Expected results

flutter for web
When restricting user input to numbers, switching the input method to Chinese should not affect the already entered content.
When the maximum limit is set, users should not be allowed to input more characters than the maximum limit.


### Actual results

flutter for web
First, I created an input field that restricts users to enter only numbers and has a maximum length of 8 characters.
    When I switch the input to Chinese, I observed that although Chinese characters cannot be entered, the existing numbers in the input field are being deleted by entering a letter. Why is this happening?
    There is another issue: even though I have set the maximum length to 8 characters, it is still exceeding the limit.


### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
 class CustomTextField extends StatelessWidget {
  final double width;
  final double height;
  final bool allowDecimal;
  final TextEditingController controller;
  final Function(String)? onSubmitted; // 用于处理回车事件

  CustomTextField({this.width = 50, this.height = 30, this.allowDecimal = false,required this.controller,this.onSubmitted});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    List<TextInputFormatter> inputFormatters = [];
    if (allowDecimal) {
      inputFormatters = [FilteringTextInputFormatter.allow(RegExp(r'^\d+\.?\d{0,2}'))];
    } else {
      inputFormatters = [FilteringTextInputFormatter.digitsOnly];
    }

    return Container(
      width: width,
      height: height,
      alignment: Alignment.center,
      child: TextField(
        controller: controller,
        inputFormatters: inputFormatters,
        style: const TextStyle(fontSize: 12.0), // 设置文字大小
        cursorWidth: 1.5, // 设置光标宽度
        maxLength: 8,
        textAlign: TextAlign.center,
        decoration: InputDecoration(
          contentPadding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 2),
          border: OutlineInputBorder(
            borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(5.0),
          ),
        ),
        onSubmitted: onSubmitted,
      ),
    );
  }
}


CustomTextField(
          width: 300,
          height: 80,
          allowDecimal: true,
          controller: TextEditingController(),
        )
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>
 
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/31436497/ab565264-a2b0-4743-b00e-3ede36cfe5ec


</details>


```console
No Log.
```


</details>

issues.title: Web: Autofill doesn't populate fields with TouchID enabled
comments:
Hi @timbera 
I wonder which Safari's configuration can show the prompt like your demo. I added a password for localhost site and the popup doesn't appear:

![Screenshot 2024-08-01 at 12 42 20](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f52aa10-02a9-4290-b28a-76923eec1223)

Maybe related to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/145101 or https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/127694

Have you enabled TouchId for autofill in you Mac's settings? Also on your screenshot I don't see any username recommendations in a dropdown that you could choose from. Did you add email and username for localhost?

It is not only Safari issue, I can recreate similar in Chrome. Although in Chrome you are asked about biometrics only once, so with the second attempt, when the prompt is not shown, user can log in. In Safari he/she stays in a loop.

Steps to reproduce on Chrome: (I will add it to the description as well)

1. Enable Touch ID for autofilling passwords on your Mac.
2. In Chrome open Google Password Manager -> Settings -> Make sure that "Use your screen lock when filling in passwords" is turned on and "Sign in automatically" is turned off. 
3. Re-open browser
4. Run the code on the web using Chrome
5. Save username & password for this user and localhost and try to login again using suggestions from the password manager. 
6. A Touch ID popup will appear. Provide your biometrics or password.

You can see several issues: First - you need to click more than once to get any hints (known issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/66356) , then after providing biometrics for TouchId, nothing happens like in Safari case. On the second attempt thought, when I'm not asked for the TouchID I'm able to prefill the fields with data. 
That makes me think that's it's about lost focus of the field. 


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7689b1c-d690-4986-a3ca-0b297831df1c

Thanks for providing more info on steps. I can reproduce this now on both Safari and Chrome browsers. 

On Safari, I also need to try many times until browser prompts a dialog asking to save password for website (localhost). 

On Chrome, I need to disable `Sign in automatically` from Google Password Manager settings:

<details open>
<summary>Chrome settings</summary>

![Screenshot 2024-08-02 at 17 18 14](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8198ae07-260e-4889-8590-91ccc438bb82)

</details>

Reproduced this on Flutter master channel as well:

```console
Flutter (Channel master, 3.24.0-1.0.pre.410, on macOS 14.6 23G80 darwin-x64, locale en-VN)
```

We have the same issue, and is keeping us stuck on Flutter 3.16.9 which is when it last worked properly.
This is still a problem in Flutter 3.24.

Could this issue be prioritised in a hot fix to 3.24 please? We really need to upgrade our product from 3.16.9 and haven't been able to use any new Flutter features in our product because of this.
This might be because the lazy-rendered `<input>` element is removed when the focus moves from the text field to the TouchID pop-up.

>In the meantime, could someone try this with semantics enabled?

If I enable semantics, the previously saved username and password will not be suggested, and thus the touch ID popup will not appear.

Has there been any progress on resolving this issue? I've seen a few Flutter bug fixes updates recently but the issue still persists.
This is a big problem for us and blocking from updating to the latest Flutter versions.
I just wanted to add that I encountered the same issue when I had FaceID enabled on my smartphone and accessed the flutter web on mobile. So it feels like it is all biometrics, not only TouchId.

Update, this is still an ongoing issue. In our web application this bug goes so far as to prevent the user from typing in the password field entirely, and instead makes all keystrokes in the email field even if the password field is focused. I'm sure this is slightly related to our code, but it is definitely related to Safari's password manager as there are no issues with Chrome/Firefox.

issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. use scaffold and set resizeToAvoidBottomInset = true;
2. use column and text field
3. call the keyboard
*** and the scaffold is not rebuilt 
------------------------
do the solution from #126585 
1. use scaffold and set resizeToAvoidBottomInset = true;
2. wrap it inside ScreenUtilInit
3. use column and text field
4. call the keyboard

it does not work for me too, I don't know if I do something wrong or either.
### Expected results

when the keyboard appears and the widget has to be rebuilt.

### Actual results

the widget is not rebuilt so that every widget is behind the keyboard

### Code sample

```dart
    return const Scaffold(
      resizeToAvoidBottomInset: true,
      body: Column(

          TextField(),
        ],

    );
```

```dart
    return ScreenUtilInit(
```

issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce 1. Add Internationalizing to the app by flutter_localizations and intl (https://docs.flutter.dev/ui/accessibility-and-internationalization/internationalization) 2. Add spellCheckConfiguration parameter to the TextField 3. Try to write wrong words ### Expected results Spell check works with AppLocalization on Android and iOS ### Actual results Spell check doesn't work with AppLocalization on iOS (there is no problem on Android) ### Code sample
Code sample https://github.com/tatraef/spell_bug.git
### Screenshots or Video
Screenshots / Video demonstration
### Logs _No response_ ### Flutter Doctor output
Doctor output ```console Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v): [✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.22.2, on macOS 14.5 23F79 darwin-arm64, locale en-RU) [✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 34.0.0) [✓] Xcode - develop for iOS and macOS (Xcode 15.4) [✓] Chrome - develop for the web [✓] Android Studio (version 2022.3) [✓] VS Code (version 1.81.1) [✓] Connected device (5 available) [✓] Network resources ```
comments: Thanks for the report @tatraef Is your app set to any other locale or is your keyboard has any other language support with which you are trying to input and it doesn't show the corrections ? I tried as below which shows the suggestions: Also check if [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138139) resembles your case or not.

@darshankawar [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138139) problem when, for example, in Russian, the correct words in English are also identified as erroneous, I also have, but my ticket is about another, with AppLocalizations spell checking does not work on iOS at all.

I found some solution, in _performSpellCheck method, in EditableText, this line
`final Locale? localeForSpellChecking = widget.locale ?? Localizations.maybeLocaleOf(context);`
returns "ru" and "en", but probably iOS native method needs codes with country code, like "ru_RU" and "en_US" 
Workaround here may be to add this line
`preferred-supported-locales: [ru, ru_RU, en, en_US]`
to l10n.yaml

Thanks for the update. I was able to replicate the reported behavior.

<details>

> I found some solution, in _performSpellCheck method, in EditableText, this line `final Locale? localeForSpellChecking = widget.locale ?? Localizations.maybeLocaleOf(context);` returns "ru" and "en", but probably iOS native method needs codes with country code, like "ru_RU" and "en_US"

@justinmc The only place I can think of is what @tatraef pointed out; that's where we decide what localization the corrections are configured to ([source](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/c9ee9ad6165f6aa232ffe579c2ea95586a46500a/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L4114)). We would need to debug further because I see code there that does the Dart --> iOS localization code translation as described.

I had similar problem as above. In my case problem was related to fact that in custom localization I have provided locale as Locale("en") instead of Locale("en", "US").

iOS class UITextChecker is using full names of languages.

issues.title: CharacterActivator not working with hardware keyboard on iOS
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

[`CharacterActivator`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/CharacterActivator-class.html) is not triggered in a Flutter app when using a hardware keyboard connected to an iOS device or simulator. It works on Android.

When testing on physical devices I used an MX Keys keyboard connected via Bluetooth. For the iOS simulator test, I used the built-in keyboard on the host machine.

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Observe the text "Ask a question"
3. Hold down Shift and press the "/" key to produce the character "?"

**Expected results:**
Screen shows "Good question!" because `CharacterActivator` triggered.

**Actual results:**
On Android, it works. On iOS, the screen continues to show "Ask a question"

You can edit the code sample to try to detect other characters and it still doesn't work on iOS.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--

```dart
}
```

issues.body: 【IOS】Long press the button and the external keyboard does not respond to continuous input
comments: Hi @kutian, Thanks for filing the issue. Please update the issue description to clearly describe the issue and please include a minimal and complete code sample along with the output of `flutter doctor -v` too.

We used flutter in the development of iOS App. Recently, when adapting to iPadOS, we found that if you use an external keyboard (such as Magic Keyboard), you cannot input text normally when you press and hold a key for continuous input. We consider that flutter can only input text by recognizing the keyboard button is pressed and bounced once, how should I solve it? Thank you.

I'm sorry that my description was inaccurate. We use an external keyboard (Bluetooth keyboard), and sometimes we need to long press the DELETE button to delete a large piece of text continuously. issues.title: After editing TextField, app auto focuses on back button; instead of next available a11y element on iOS
issues.body:
See internal b/151685565.

Steps to Reproduce:

- Construct a page with a TextField and a Button
```
    const Text('Testing TextField A11y'),
    TextField(controller: TextEditingController()),
    FilledButton(onPressed: () {  },child: const Text('button'),),
```

- Double-Tap TextField to edit it
- Then focus on done key on the keyboard, and double tap
The keyboard will dismiss, then it will focus on the back button by default, not the next button.
comments:
After further investigation, I'm thinking this _might_ be working as intended.

I wasn't able to find many apps which implement this pattern on iOS, most apps seem to force you to make a selection instead of hitting return on the keyboard, or immediately take you to a new page after hitting return based on what you typed. Those that let you not make a selection and keep you on the same page would refocus on the first element on the page.

Example: searching in Gmail on iOS.

1. Select and activate "Search in mail" at the top.
    1. This will bring up the search pane and open the input box.
2. Select and activate the "Search" button on the keyboard.
3. The back button (aka: the first element on the page) is now focused.
    1. If the next element was chosen to be focused, we would be focusing on the input for voice search, which wouldn't make sense.

As seen above, it's not always clear where the focus should go next, and jumping to the next element may not be desirable in all cases.

**Requested further clarification from customer on internal ticket. Leaving this issue open for now.**

This slipped, and I'm unable to follow-up due to returning to school.

As noted above I wasn't able to find a native app which implements this behavior, but I am now unsure about my initial guess of WAI. This may be a real issue, but will need further investigation.

issues.title: Windows desktop app completely losing keyboard focus
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

Rarely it happens with windows desktop app, that the application completely stops being responsive to any hardware keyboard input. I managed to discover only one scenario where the issue can be reliably reproduced.

1. create a new project, e.g. flutter create test_app --platforms windows
2. add a new TextField widget
3. build and run
4. press Win+D to minimize all apps
5. press Win + D to regain focus
6. try writting into the TextField

Input appears in the TextField

issues.title: Composing characters (e.g. Umlauts) using physical keyboard on iOS generates two characters

I started investigating this, but paused as @LongCatIsLooong is working on a prototype that should address this.

I'l dump my notes here in case we don't move forward with that prototype.

On iOS, pressing `ALT-u + u` on an external keyboard should produce `ü`. However, it produces `¨ü`:

1. I press `ALT-u`
2. This calls [`[FlutterTextInputView setMarkedText:selectedRange:]`](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/619d985931ba8171cafdf2e3f7ec10566b7e444a/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/macos/framework/Source/FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm#L847)
3. The framework receives a `TextInputClient.updateEditingState` message with value `¨`
4. I press `u`
5. This calls [`[FlutterTextInputView insertText:]`](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/619d985931ba8171cafdf2e3f7ec10566b7e444a/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/macos/framework/Source/FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm#L741)
6. The framework receives a `TextInputClient.updateEditingState` message with value `¨ü`

Apple's [Handling text interactions in custom keyboards](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/handling-text-interactions-in-custom-keyboards?language=objc#Mark-text-while-the-user-is-editing) docs state:

> Calling `setMarkedText` selects the text specified by range. Subsequent insertions replace the characters selected in the range. In the example above, a space with a combining acute accent is shown. If the next character input by the user is an a, you could then insert a combined á.

This indicates that `[FlutterTextInputView insertText:]` needs to be updated to replace the characters that were selected by `[FlutterTextInputView setMarkedText:selectedRange:]`'s range.
